keyMetrics.capitalReturn: Dividend raised 4% to $0.27/share; new $100B buyback authorization

aiCommentary: Apple positioned 2026 as its catch-up year on AI. Management pointed to the revamped, Gemini-powered Siri rolling out in iOS 26.5 (mid-2026) with a fuller conversational version targeted for iOS 27 in September, leaning on a multiyear ~$1B/year Google partnership while emphasizing on-device processing and Private Cloud Compute for privacy. The narrative remains that Apple is a fast-follower in generative AI rather than a leader, and investors are watching execution on the delayed Siri timeline closely.
demandCommentary: Demand was the standout: Cook described iPhone 17 demand as 'off the charts' and the 'most popular lineup in our history,' with iPhone setting a March-quarter record despite supply constraints on advanced TSMC processor nodes and a global memory shortage. All five product categories and all five geographic segments grew, with Greater China rebounding sharply (+28%). Management guided June-quarter revenue up 14-17% YoY but cautioned that memory costs will be 'significantly higher' next quarter as memory makers redirect capacity to AI chips, a headwind to forward gross margin.

keyQuotes.quote: Today Apple is proud to report our best March quarter ever, with revenue of $111.2 billion and double-digit growth across every geographic segment.
keyQuotes.speaker: Tim Cook, CEO

keyQuotes.quote: iPhone achieved a March quarter revenue record, fueled by such extraordinary demand for the iPhone 17 lineup.
keyQuotes.speaker: Tim Cook, CEO

keyQuotes.quote: Our strong business performance during the March quarter generated over $28 billion in operating cash flow and drove new March quarter records for both operating cash flow and EPS.
keyQuotes.speaker: Kevan Parekh, CFO

keyQuotes.quote: There's just a little less flexibility in the supply chain at the moment in terms of getting more parts available.
keyQuotes.speaker: Tim Cook, CEO
